methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: convert
methods.description: Creates a new body where the faces and edges are converted to different types of geometry based on the input options. This is particularly useful when you need a body made up entirely of NURBS surfaces.   The tempId on the faces, edges, and vertices on the new body will match with the corresponding tempId on the original body. In cases where faces are split as a result of the conversion there can be more than one face or edge in the new body that matches to a single face or edge in the original body. The findByTempId method will find the entity with the matching id.

methods.name: convertToSheetMetal
methods.description: Converts the current BRepBody to a sheet metal body. This is applicable only if the body is not already a sheet metal body (isSheetMetal is false).

methods.name: copy
methods.description: Copies the body to the clipboard.   This property is only valid if the IsTransient property is false.

methods.name: copyToComponent
methods.description: Creates a copy of this body into the specified target.

methods.name: createComponent
methods.description: Creates a new component and occurrence within the component that currently owns this body. This body is moved into the new component and returned. The newly created component can be obtained by using the parentComponent property of the BRepBody object.   This method is only valid if the IsTransient property is false.

methods.name: createForAssemblyContext
methods.description: Returns a proxy for the native object - i.e. a new object that represents this object but adds the assembly context defined by the input occurrence.   This method is only valid if the IsTransient property is false.

methods.name: cut
methods.description: Cuts the body to the clipboard.   This property is only valid if the IsTransient property is false.

methods.name: deleteMe
methods.description: Deletes the body.   This property is only valid if the IsTransient property is false.

methods.name: findByTempId
methods.description: Returns all of the faces, edges, or vertices that match the input ID.

methods.name: findThicknessAtFace
methods.description: Finds the thickness of the body at the specified face by casting a ray from the face along its inward normal and measuring the distance to the first opposite face of the body that is hit.

methods.name: getPhysicalProperties
methods.description: Returns the PhysicalProperties object that has properties for getting the area, density, mass, volume, moments, etc of this body.

methods.name: moveToComponent
methods.description: Moves this body from its current component into the root component or the component owned by the specified occurrence.

methods.name: pointContainment
methods.description: Determines the relationship of the input point with respect to this body.

properties.name: appearance
properties.description: Read-write property that gets and sets the current appearance of the body. Setting this property will result in applying an override appearance to the body and the AppearanceSourceType property will return OverrideAppearanceSource. Setting this property to null will remove any override.   This property is only valid if the IsTransient property is false.

properties.name: appearanceSourceType
properties.description: Read-write property that gets the source of the appearance for the body. If this returns OverrideAppearanceSource, an override exists on this body. The override can be removed by setting the Appearance property to null.   This property is only valid if the IsTransient property is false.

properties.name: area
properties.description: Returns the area in cm ^ 2.

properties.name: assemblyContext
properties.description: Returns the assembly occurrence (i.e. the occurrence) of this object in an assembly. This is only valid in the case where this BRepBody object is acting as a proxy in an assembly. Returns null in the case where the object is not in the context of an assembly, but is already the native object. Also returns null in the case where this body is transient.

properties.name: attributes
properties.description: Returns the collection of attributes associated with this face.

properties.name: baseFeature
properties.description: If this body is associated with a base feature, this property will return that base feature. If it's not associated with a base feature, this property will return null.

properties.name: boundingBox
properties.description: Returns the bounding box of this body.

properties.name: concaveEdges
properties.description: Returns all of the edges that connect concave faces.

properties.name: convexEdges
properties.description: Returns all of the edges that connect convex faces.

properties.name: deriveFeature
properties.description: Returns the DeriveFeature if this BRepBody is derived from another design. This property returns null if the BRepBody is not derived from another design (i.e. isDerived property returns false).

properties.name: edges
properties.description: Returns a collection of all of the edges in the body.

properties.name: entityToken
properties.description: Returns a token for the BRepBody object. This can be saved and used at a later time with the Design.findEntityByToken method to get back the same body.   When using entity tokens it's important to understand that the token string returned for a specific entity can be different over time. However, even if you have two different token strings that were obtained from the same entity, when you use findEntityByToken they will both return the same entity. Because of that you should never compare entity tokens as way to determine what the token represents. Instead, you need to use the findEntityByToken method to get the two entities identified by the tokens and then compare them.   This is only valid for bodies that exist in the design, (the isTemporary property is false).

properties.name: faces
properties.description: Returns a collection of all of the faces in the body.

properties.name: isDerived
properties.description: Returns if this BRepBody is derived from another design. If true, this body cannot be deleted.

properties.name: isLightBulbOn
properties.description: Gets and sets if the light bulb beside the body node in the browser is on or not. Parent nodes in the browser can have their light bulb off which affects all of their children so this property does not indicate if the body is actually visible, just that it should be visible if all of its parent nodes are also visible. Use the isVisible property to determine if it's actually visible.   This property is only valid if the IsTransient property is false.

properties.name: isSelectable
properties.description: Gets and sets if this body is selectable.   This property is only valid if the IsTransient property is false.

properties.name: isSheetMetal
properties.description: Indicates if this body represents a sheet metal folded part or not and if a flat pattern can be created.

properties.name: isSolid
properties.description: Returns whether this body is closed (solid) or not.

properties.name: isTemporary
properties.description: Indicates if this body is represented in the model or is temporary.

properties.name: isTransient
properties.description: Indicates if this body is represented in the model or is transient.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: isVisible
properties.description: Gets if this body is currently visible in the graphics window. Use the isLightBulbOn to change if the light bulb beside the body node in the browser is on or not. Parent nodes in the browser can have their light bulb off which affects all of their children. This property indicates the final result and whether this body is actually visible or not.   This property is only valid if the IsTransient property is false.

properties.name: lumps
properties.description: Returns a collection of all of the lumps in the body.

properties.name: material
properties.description: Gets and sets the material assigned to this body.   This property is only valid if the IsTransient property is false.

properties.name: meshManager
properties.description: Returns the mesh manager object for this body.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Gets and sets the name of the body.   This property is only valid if the IsTransient property is false.

properties.name: nativeObject
properties.description: The NativeObject is the object outside the context of an assembly. Returns null in the case where this object is not in the context of an assembly but is already the native object.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: opacity
properties.description: Gets and sets the opacity override assigned to this body. A value of 1.0 specifies that it is completely opaque and a value of 0.0 specifies that it is completely transparent.   This value is not necessarily related to what the user sees because the opacity is inherited. For example, if this body is in a component and that component's opacity is set to something other than 1.0, the body will also be shown as slightly transparent even though the opacity property for the body will return 1.0. Because the component that contains the body can be referenced as an occurrence in other components and they can have different opacity settings, it's possible that different instances of the same body can display using different opacity levels. To get the opacity that it is being displayed with use the BRepBody.visibleOpacity property.   This is the API equivalent of the "Opacity Control" command available for the body in the browser.

properties.name: orientedMinimumBoundingBox
properties.description: Returns an oriented bounding box of the body that is best oriented to tightly fit the body.

properties.name: parentComponent
properties.description: Returns the component this body is owned by.

properties.name: physicalProperties
properties.description: Returns the PhysicalProperties object that has properties for getting the area, density, mass, volume, moments, etc of this body. Property values will be calculated using the 'LowCalculationAccuracy' setting when using this property to get the PhysicalProperties object. To specify a higher calculation tolerance, use the getPhysicalProperties method on the Design class instead.

properties.name: preciseBoundingBox
properties.description: Returns a bounding box that tightly fits this body.

properties.name: revisionId
properties.description: Returns the current revision ID of the body. This ID changes any time the body is modified in any way. By getting and saving the ID when you create any data that is dependent on the body, you can then compare the saved ID with the current ID to determine if the body has changed to know if you should update your data.

properties.name: shells
properties.description: Returns a collection of all of the shells in the body.

properties.name: textureMapControl
properties.description: Returns the TextureMapControl object associated with this body when there is an appearance assigned to the body that has a texture associated with it. If there isn't a texture, this property will return null. If there is a texture, you can use the returned object to query and modify how the texture is applied to the body.

properties.name: vertices
properties.description: Returns a collection of all of the vertices in the body.

properties.name: visibleOpacity
properties.description: The user can set an override opacity for components and bodies. These opacity overrides combine if children and parent components have overrides. This property returns the actual opacity that is being used to render the body. To set the opacity use the opacity property of the BRepBody object.

properties.name: volume
properties.description: Returns the volume in cm ^ 3. Returns 0 in the case the body is not solid.

properties.name: wires
properties.description: Returns any wire bodies that exist within this body.

related_samples.name: Get Volume of Active Design API Sample
related_samples.description: Traverses through the active design and totals the volume of every body within the design.

appearance.title: BRepBody.appearance Property
appearance.description: Read-write property that gets and sets the current appearance of the body. Setting this property will result in applying an override appearance to the body and the AppearanceSourceType property will return OverrideAppearanceSource. Setting this property to null will remove any override. This property is only valid if the IsTransient property is false.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = bRepBody_var.appearance

# Set the value of the property.
bRepBody_var.appearance = propertyValue

related_samples.name: Material API Sample
related_samples.description:
Demonstrates using materials and appearance using the API.
To use the sample, create a new Python or C++ script and copy and paste this code, replacing the default code. The sample also used an external appearance library which you can get here. Copy that to any location on your computer and edit the path in the script. When running the script, have a design open that contains a body in the root component.

classType.title: BRepBody.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

syntax.python: returnValue = adsk.fusion.BRepBody.classType()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = adsk::fusion::BRepBody::classType();

classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

convert.title: BRepBody.convert Method
convert.description: Creates a new body where the faces and edges are converted to different types of geometry based on the input options. This is particularly useful when you need a body made up entirely of NURBS surfaces. The tempId on the faces, edges, and vertices on the new body will match with the corresponding tempId on the original body. In cases where faces are split as a result of the conversion there can be more than one face or edge in the new body that matches to a single face or edge in the original body. The findByTempId method will find the entity with the matching id.

syntax.python: returnValue = bRepBody_var.convert(options)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = bRepBody_var->convert(options);

convert.return_description: Returns the new converted body or null in the case of failure.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: Input options that define how the conversion should be done. These are bitwise options so they can be combined.

copy.title: BRepBody.copy Method
copy.description: Copies the body to the clipboard. This property is only valid if the IsTransient property is false.

syntax.python: returnValue = bRepBody_var.copy()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = bRepBody_var->copy();

copy.return_description: Returns true if the copy was successful.

copyToComponent.title: BRepBody.copyToComponent Method
copyToComponent.description: Creates a copy of this body into the specified target.

syntax.python: returnValue = bRepBody_var.copyToComponent(target)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = bRepBody_var->copyToComponent(target);

copyToComponent.return_description: Returns the moved BRepBody or null in the case the move failed.

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: The target can be either the root component or an occurrence.   In the case where an occurrence is specified, the body will be copied into the parent component of the target occurrence and the target occurrence defines the transform of how the body will be copied so that the body maintains it's same position with respect to the assembly.   If target is null, then a copy of the body is created in the owning component of the original body.

createComponent.title: BRepBody.createComponent Method
createComponent.description: Creates a new component and occurrence within the component that currently owns this body. This body is moved into the new component and returned. The newly created component can be obtained by using the parentComponent property of the BRepBody object. This method is only valid if the IsTransient property is false.

syntax.python: returnValue = bRepBody_var.createComponent()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = bRepBody_var->createComponent();

createComponent.return_description: Returns the BRrepBody in the new component or null in the case the creation failed.

createForAssemblyContext.title: BRepBody.createForAssemblyContext Method
createForAssemblyContext.description: Returns a proxy for the native object - i.e. a new object that represents this object but adds the assembly context defined by the input occurrence. This method is only valid if the IsTransient property is false.

syntax.python: returnValue = bRepBody_var.createForAssemblyContext(occurrence)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = bRepBody_var->createForAssemblyContext(occurrence);

createForAssemblyContext.return_description: Returns the new BRepBoy proxy or null if this isn't the NativeObject.

parameters.name: occurrence
parameters.description: The occurrence that defines the context for the created proxy.

cut.title: BRepBody.cut Method
cut.description: Cuts the body to the clipboard. This property is only valid if the IsTransient property is false.

syntax.python: returnValue = bRepBody_var.cut()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = bRepBody_var->cut();

cut.return_description: Returns true if the cut was successful.

deleteMe.title: BRepBody.deleteMe Method
deleteMe.description: Deletes the body. This property is only valid if the IsTransient property is false.

syntax.python: returnValue = bRepBody_var.deleteMe()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = bRepBody_var->deleteMe();

deleteMe.return_description: Returns true if the delete was successful.

findByTempId.title: BRepBody.findByTempId Method
findByTempId.description: Returns all of the faces, edges, or vertices that match the input ID.

syntax.python: returnValue = bRepBody_var.findByTempId(tempId)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = bRepBody_var->findByTempId(tempId);

findByTempId.return_description: Returns an array of entities that have the specified ID. This returns an array because it's possible that a body created by converting a body can have multiple entities with the same ID in the case where a curve or face was split. Returns an empty array in the case where no match is found.

parameters.name: tempId
parameters.description: The ID of the B-Rep entity to find.

getPhysicalProperties.title: BRepBody.getPhysicalProperties Method
getPhysicalProperties.description: Returns the PhysicalProperties object that has properties for getting the area, density, mass, volume, moments, etc of this body.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = bRepBody_var.getPhysicalProperties()

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = bRepBody_var.getPhysicalProperties(accuracy)

getPhysicalProperties.return_description: Returns a PhysicalProperties object that can be used to get the various physical property related values.

parameters.name: accuracy
parameters.description: Specifies the desired level of computational accuracy of the property calculations. The default value of 'LowCalculationAccuracy' returns results within a +/- 1% error margin.   This is an optional argument whose default value is CalculationAccuracy.LowCalculationAccuracy.

moveToComponent.title: BRepBody.moveToComponent Method
moveToComponent.description: Moves this body from it's current component into the root component or the component owned by the specified occurrence.

syntax.python: returnValue = bRepBody_var.moveToComponent(target)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = bRepBody_var->moveToComponent(target);

moveToComponent.return_description: Returns the moved BRepBody or null in the case the move failed.

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: The target can be either the root component or an occurrence.   In the case where an occurrence is specified, the body will be moved into the parent component of the target occurrence and the target occurrence defines the transform of how the body will be copied so that the body maintains it's same position with respect to the assembly.

pointContainment.title: BRepBody.pointContainment Method
pointContainment.description: Determines the relationship of the input point with respect to this body.

syntax.python: returnValue = bRepBody_var.pointContainment(point)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = bRepBody_var->pointContainment(point);

pointContainment.return_description: Returns a value from the PointContainment enum indicating the relationship of the input point to the body.

parameters.name: point
parameters.description: The point to do the containment check for.

convertToSheetMetal.title: BRepBody.convertToSheetMetal Method
convertToSheetMetal.description: Converts the current BRepBody to a sheet metal body. This is applicable only if the body is not already a sheet metal body (isSheetMetal is false).

syntax.python: returnValue = bRepBody_var.convertToSheetMetal(baseFace, sheetMetalRule)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = bRepBody_var->convertToSheetMetal(baseFace, sheetMetalRule);

convertToSheetMetal.return_description: Returns true if the conversion was successful. Returns false in the case of failure.

parameters.name: baseFace
parameters.description: Specifies a BRepFace that will be used to determine the thickness of the body.

parameters.name: sheetMetalRule
parameters.description: If the parent component is a standard component, this method will convert it to a sheet metal component. The specified design or library sheet metal rule will be copied and applied as the active sheet metal rule of the sheet metal component. The Thickness value of the copied sheet metal rule will be changed to the thickness determined by the baseFace of this BRepBody. If the parent component is already a sheet metal component, this argument will be ignored and the active sheet metal rule will be used.

related_samples.name: Convert To Sheet Metal Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates finding the thickness of a body at a face and converting a BRepBody to sheet metal.

findThicknessAtFace.title: BRepBody.findThicknessAtFace Method
findThicknessAtFace.description: Finds the thickness of the body at the specified face by casting a ray from the face along its inward normal and measuring the distance to the first opposite face of the body that is hit.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
(returnValue, thickness) = bRepBody_var.findThicknessAtFace(face)

# Uses optional arguments.
(returnValue, thickness) = bRepBody_var.findThicknessAtFace(face, hitPoint)

findThicknessAtFace.return_description: Returns true if the thickness was successfully determined. Returns false if it could not be determined, for example if the face is non-planar or has no parallel opposite face.

parameters.name: face
parameters.description: Specifies a planar BRepFace on the body from which to measure the thickness.

parameters.name: thickness
parameters.description: The output thickness value in centimeters.

parameters.name: hitPoint
parameters.description: An optional Point3D that specifies the point on the face from which to cast the ray. The point must lie on the given face. If not specified, a point is automatically chosen on the face.   This is an optional argument whose default value is null.

related_samples.name: Convert To Sheet Metal Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates finding the thickness of a body at a face and converting a BRepBody to sheet metal.
